title: Best Value-for-Money Winner
insight: Aristocrat delivers the highest sentiment-to-price ratio (VFM score: 0.782), meaning customers feel they get the most satisfaction per rupee spent. In contrast, American Tourister scores lowest (0.383), suggesting customers expect more for the price they pay.

title: Discount-Dependent Brands
insight: Safari, Aristocrat rely on heavy discounting (>50% average) to drive sales. This suggests their list prices are artificially inflated for perceived value, or their brand equity isn't strong enough to command full price. A decision-maker should question whether these brands can sustain margins.

title: The Wheel Quality Gap
insight: Wheel quality is the most polarizing aspect across brands. Aristocrat leads with sentiment score 0.769 while Safari scores 0.208. Since wheels are the #1 mentioned aspect in negative reviews, this single feature significantly impacts overall brand perception.

title: Industry-Wide Durability Concerns
insight: Durability is mentioned in 183 reviews across all brands, making it the most discussed concern in the luggage category. No brand has solved this completely, presenting an opportunity for a brand that can credibly communicate and deliver long-term durability.
